claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, cross-application drag/drop, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria DropZone supplies accessible drop-target semantics and contains a real native FileTrigger alternative.
semantics.naming: A non-empty visible label names the drop target; visible description and live status are connected as descriptions.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role, name, drop, paste, and children overrides are outside the supported contract.

interaction.keyboard: Tab reaches the drop target and native choose-files control; React Aria accessible drop mode and platform file picker remain available.
interaction.pointer: Files may be dropped or selected through the visible native control; drop-target state appears only for a valid active drag.
interaction.touch: The choose-files path is always present because mobile browsers may not expose desktop drag/drop.
interaction.speech: Visible dropzone and choose-files labels provide distinct voice targets.
interaction.switchAccess: The native file control is reachable independently of drag/drop mode.

formBehavior: Optional name, form, and required props participate through the one native FileTrigger. Picker, paste, and drop synchronize only accepted Files into its FileList without synthetic input/change events; failure clears the input and reports formDataSynchronized=false. Native reset restores idle status and invokes onReset without fabricating change.

states.synchronization: Disabled/busy affordances, latest-request ownership, accepted/rejected result, visible status, and polite announcement derive from one bounded classifier.

preferences.enhancedContrast: A dashed-to-solid boundary and outline communicate target state beyond color.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses Canvas, CanvasText, Highlight, and GrayText with forced-color adjustment enabled.
preferences.reducedMotion: Drop, busy, accepted, and rejected states do not depend on motion.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Labels, descriptions, status, and native trigger wrap within the surface at narrow widths.

internationalization.imePolicy: Paste handling consumes only clipboard files and does not interfere with ordinary text when no FileList is present.
internationalization.serialization: File objects remain local browser objects; rejection summaries expose only name, type, size, and a bounded reason.

consumerResponsibilities: Explain accepted type, count, size, privacy, and transport policy in persistent visible copy.
Validate file signatures and server policy again before preview or upload; browser MIME and extension values are not trust boundaries.
Treat formDataSynchronized=false as a blocked direct-form submission and keep visible recovery available.
Treat accepted files as a selection result, never consent to upload.
Render detailed per-file rejection recovery in the owning FileUpload queue when more than a summary is needed.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include packed-consumer, cross-application drag/drop, package/source parity, or manual assistive-technology evidence.
Directories and non-file drag items are rejected rather than recursively enumerated; directory upload is a separate explicit product mode.
